text: Electrical installations shall be designed and constructed to provide protection against electric shock, fire, thermal effects, and overcurrent in accordance with AS/NZS 3000. All circuits shall be protected by appropriate circuit protection devices.

A main earthing conductor shall connect the main earthing terminal to the earthing electrode. The minimum size shall be 4.0 mm² for copper conductors in normal installations. Larger sizes may be required for high fault current applications.

Switchboards shall be installed in locations that are readily accessible for operation and maintenance. The minimum working space in front of the switchboard shall be 1000mm width and 600mm depth, with adequate headroom of not less than 2000mm.

Circuit breakers protecting final sub-circuits for socket outlets shall have a rated current not exceeding 20A for single phase and 32A for three phase circuits. The installation must comply with maximum demand calculations.

Underground cables shall be installed at a minimum depth of 450mm below finished ground level in areas not subject to vehicular traffic, and 600mm in areas subject to vehicular traffic. Warning tape shall be placed 300mm above the cable.

Maximum voltage drop from origin to any point in the installation shall not exceed 5% for lighting circuits and 5% for other circuits under normal load conditions. Calculations must account for cable length, load current, and conductor size.

Emergency lighting shall be provided in all exit paths, exit signs must be illuminated at all times when the building is occupied. Battery backup systems must maintain operation for not less than 90 minutes during mains failure.

Residual current devices (RCDs) with rated residual operating current not exceeding 30mA shall be provided for all socket outlets intended for connection of portable equipment outdoors or in wet areas. Testing frequency: monthly.
